uses: Tragia triandra has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa, for its various health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In addition, Tragia triandra is also used to treat wounds and ulcers due to its antiseptic properties. The plant's extracts have also been shown to have antioxidant and antifungal activities, which can help in protecting against cell damage and infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential of Tragia triandra.

intro: Tragia triandra is a plant species commonly known as the "Indian numbers" or "stinging nettle" due to its ability to cause skin irritation when touched. The plant has been reported to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antidiabetic properties. The bioactive constituents of Tragia triandra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Tragia triandra is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
